_comment: Pages watched monthly by scripts/watch_sources.py. A change opens/updates a report for a maintainer to TRIAGE (it may be a new/amended instrument OR a cosmetic page update). Nothing is ingested automatically. Per-source fields: capture_type (pdf|html|json) hints how the crawl layer captures the listed documents; scope tags the design-lock scope area; add an OPTIONAL "schema" (a file under scripts/crawl/schemas/) to switch a source from whole-page-hash to structured schema-mode monitoring (see scripts/crawl/README.md). [Expanded 2026-07-04: Mining Code drafts/standards/guidance, LTC, 31st session, ITLOS case list, US Federal Register DSM feed + NOAA. 2026-07: capture_type/scope added for the Crawl4AI source-mapping layer.]

sources._note: 2026-07-18: was named 'Council documents' and pointed at /sessions/, which SILENTLY REDIRECTS to an agenda-item page from a past session (sessions-30th-session-2025-item16). There is no /sessions/ index — sessions are listed only in the site nav. It had already flagged one meaningless 'change' as the redirect target drifted. Repointed to the most recent completed session; the current session has its own source. NEEDS ANNUAL REVIEW when a new session opens. Hash cleared to force a fresh baseline.

sources.name: ITLOS — Case No. 17 (2011 Advisory Opinion)
US eCFR — 15 CFR Part 970 (NOAA deep seabed mining, exploration licences)
sources._note: 2026-07-18: the human-facing eCFR page BLOCKS automated fetches — it redirected to unblock.federalregister.gov ('Request Access'), so this source was hashing an access-denied page. Symptom visible in hindsight: parts 970 and 971 carried IDENTICAL hashes, which two different regulations cannot. Switched to the OFFICIAL eCFR versioner API, which is published for programmatic use (NOT an evasion — no stealth, no proxy, per crawl boundary rule 2). Bonus: it reports amendment dates per section, so a change names the sections amended. Document capture for ingest remains a separate, human-verified step. Hash cleared to force a fresh baseline.
